posts.content:
The US National Academies of Sciences refused to remove a climate change chapter from the Reference Manual on Scientific Evidence, despite pressure from Republican attorneys general.
https://arstechnica.com/science/2026/03/national-academies-of-sciences-resisting-pressure-to-pull-climate-info/

M5.8 earthquake, Cuba, at 04.28 UTC on 17 March 2026. P-wave detected in Nottingham using 'slinky' school seismometer
#earthquake ⚒️ #geology 🦕
https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/eventpage/us6000sgrt/executive

Here's an updated view of the 12-month running mean global temperature, which is a simple metric that provides insight on climate change and climate variability. I would expect this to begin increasing again later on in 2026/2027.
Graphic from global climate indicators: https://zacklabe.com/climate-change-indicators/

You would like to contribute directly to the IPCC 7th Assessment Report ?
📢 I am recruiting a Chapter Scientist to work with me and the author team of Working Group 3 Chapter 3, on projected futures in the context of sustainable development and climate change.
Join our amazing team on our journey to pull together the best possible chapter on sustainable and equitable low-carbon futures.
More details & application : https://www.centre-cired.fr/en/cdd-chapter-scientist-for-the-chapter-3-of-ipcc-wgiii-ar7/

Our research note with some preliminary results from JWST observations of Kreutz sungrazer C/2026 A1 (MAPS) has now been posted, ahead of the comet's perihelion next month: https://doi.org/10.3847/2515-5172/ae5220 The nucleus appears to be ~0.4 km in diameter, which is probably bigger than that of the minor Kreutz fragments routinely seen with coronagraphs, and perhaps comparable to/slightly smaller than that of C/2011 W3 (Lovejoy). However, the far stronger outgassing of C/2026 A1 at its current distance is working against its survival and might spin the nucleus apart well before perihelion.
People publishing Earth Sciences (and anything spatial, actually), I'm growing sick of sounding like a broken record - note your F@#king coordinates!
